behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: grouping key is trace ID, and service origin must not affect grouping. Hypothetical elsewhere: ingestion transport, file/network I/O, cross-process collection. MOCK-MISS: failure is in grouping logic, not inherently a module handoff failure. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not inherently hide this failure because pure input sets can expose it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration/System because behavior text only requires correct grouping by fields in provided log entries, not real infrastructure wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test a correlator/grouping function with mixed-service JSON records containing shared and distinct trace IDs; assert all same-trace records land in one group independent of service name.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: ordering and skew-handling rule (<=500ms) in timeline construction. Hypothetical elsewhere: UI rendering stack, distributed clock sync protocols. MOCK-MISS: primary failure is ordering algorithm correctness. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed to validate skew compensation rule. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic synthetic timestamps can expose failures directly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration/System because behavior text does not require external clocks or real service communication, only correct ordering logic under specified skew.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test timeline builder with crafted cross-service events whose timestamps differ within and beyond 500ms; assert resulting order follows skew policy and strict timestamp sort semantics.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: statistical threshold rule (p99 > 3x baseline) and flag emission. Hypothetical elsewhere: live traffic ingestion, persistence/report delivery channels. MOCK-MISS: failure is in percentile/baseline/threshold logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required for rule validation. MOCK-HIDE: stubs do not hide this if real numeric distributions are provided to detector. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration/System because behavior text defines a deterministic numeric condition, not infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test anomaly detector with synthetic latency distributions and baseline values around threshold boundaries (just below, equal, above 3x); assert spike flag appears only when condition is met and is included in report payload.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: parser failure must propagate as skip (not crash) and increment a reported counter. Hypothetical elsewhere: disk/network log sources, distributed retries. MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module boundaries (parser -> pipeline control flow -> metrics/reporting). REAL-INFRA: can be tested fully in-process with local modules and in-memory inputs, so System not required. MOCK-HIDE: unit-mocking parser exceptions can miss real handoff bugs in skip accounting and pipeline continuity, so Integration is needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring parser, pipeline runner, and reporting module: feed a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON lines; assert process completes, malformed lines are skipped, and skipped-count metric/report equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: sustained throughput and no-drop/no-lag under burst load, which depends on runtime scheduling, buffering, and timing under real execution conditions. Hypothetical elsewhere: external API outages, multi-cluster deployment. MOCK-MISS: in-process mocks can hide queue backpressure and scheduler timing failures. REAL-INFRA: requires real process/runtime and OS timing behavior, so System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly performance-under-burst; synthetic module-only tests may pass while real runtime drops/lags.
behaviors.test_description: System load test running the real tool process with a generator producing 10,000 structured log entries/sec for a fixed window; verify ingested count equals sent count and processing lag/backlog stays within defined non-falling-behind threshold throughout.
